Defendant Maria Carvajal, Humberto Navarro, William Johnston Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-27-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Shoshana Raphael of the law offices of Zachs Freedman & Patterson PC (shoshana@zfplaw.com) appears as counsel for moving/opposing party, plaintiff SF Mission Properties LLC

Attorney, Raquel Fox of the law offices of Tenderloin Housing Clinic (raquel@thclinic.org) appears as counsel for demurring parties, defendants

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides, on record as reflected in the notes of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendants Maria Carvajal, Humberto Navarro and William Johnston's Demurrer To Complaint for ejectment is overruled. San Francisco Administrative Code ("SFAC") sec. 37.9A(e)(3)(A) requires a landlord to make a relocation payment to an "authorized occupant" of the rental unit at the time of the service of the notice of termination of tenancy. In this case, plaintiff alleges that it served the notice to terminate on April 30, 2019. (Complaint, par. 10.) Paragraph 13 of the complaint further alleges that plaintiff first discovered that Johnston claimed to be an occupant of the premises on May 16, 2019. Plaintiff obviously could not have tendered the relocation payment to Johnston on April 30, 2019 because plaintiff did not know about him and the court takes plaintiff's allegation of ignorance as true on demurrer. Whether or not Johnston was an "authorized occupant" entitled to relocation benefits is a factual issue that cannot be resolved on demurrer. (See Danger Panda, LLC v. Launiu (2017) 10 Cal.App.5th 502, 514-516 [discussing distinctions between "lawful occupant" and "tenant"].) Moreover, paragraph 14 alleges that on May 29, 2019, plaintiff sent Johnston relocation benefits after learning of his occupancy and claimed disability. Under these circumstances, it was not error for plaintiff to characterize these payments as additional relocation payments. The court notes that the payments were timely provided based upon plaintiff's discovery of Johnston on May 16, 2019. (See SFAC sec. 37.9A(e)(3)(C) [landlord to make additional payment within 15 calendar days]; see Johnson v. City and County of San Francisco (2006) 137 Cal.App.4th 7, 18 [the tenant "is in the best position" to show eligibility for relocation payments to the landlord, who may face delays if he or she has a mistaken 'belief' about a tenant's eligibility for payment"].)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com.
